Category: Education & Public Sector: Education, Government Services, Non-Profit organizations
Who is this for: Government employees, public administrators, educators, teachers, school administrators, healthcare workers, non-profit staff, public relations officers, communications managers, policy makers, civil servants
Team or Dept this is for: Communications, Public Relations, Government Affairs, Education, Healthcare, Non-Profit Operations, Policy Development, Public Information
Description: How false information spreads faster with AI tools.
Prerequisites: Learners should have a basic familiarity with using the internet and social media, along with a general awareness of current events, but no prior knowledge of artificial intelligence or data science is required.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key ways in which AI tools accelerate the spread of false information across public institutions, and explain the common tactics used to manipulate educational, governmental, and non-profit organisations. You will also apply practical strategies to recognise AI-driven misinformation in your own professional context, helping you respond with greater confidence and clarity.
